Key Clinical Indicators: Severe chest pain, BP abnormalities, sweating, shortness of breath, radiating pain to arm/jaw
General Treatment Approach: Emergency hospitalization, oxygen therapy, antiplatelet agents, anticoagulants, percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), cardiac monitoring, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: High risk in urban India; delay in reaching tertiary care centers in rural areas

Key Clinical Indicators: Sudden confusion, severe headache, facial drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty, high BP
General Treatment Approach: Emergency CT/MRI, thrombolytic therapy (if ischemic within 4.5 hrs), antiplatelet therapy, blood pressure management, neurological monitoring, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: Rising incidence in younger Indians; time-critical treatment ('time is brain')

Key Clinical Indicators: BP >180/120 mmHg, severe headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, visual changes
General Treatment Approach: IV antihypertensive medications, continuous BP monitoring, ICU care, gradual BP reduction, organ function assessment
India-Specific Notes: Common in uncontrolled hypertension; requires immediate BP reduction

Key Clinical Indicators: Chest pain, shortness of breath, high cholesterol, abnormal ECG
General Treatment Approach: Antiplatelet therapy, statins, beta-blockers, nitrates, possible angioplasty/stenting, cardiac rehabilitation
India-Specific Notes: High prevalence in Indians; early onset (40s-50s) compared to Western populations

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe shortness of breath, rapid heart rate, swelling, inability to lie flat
General Treatment Approach: Oxygen therapy, diuretics, vasodilators, inotropic support if needed, fluid management, ICU monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Often complication of untreated hypertension or diabetes

Key Clinical Indicators: High fever, cough, shortness of breath, WBC >12000, low oxygen saturation
General Treatment Approach: Hospitalization, IV antibiotics (broad-spectrum initially), oxygen therapy, respiratory support, hydration
India-Specific Notes: Monsoon-related cases common; vaccination recommended for high-risk groups

Key Clinical Indicators: Persistent cough >3 weeks, fever, night sweats, weight loss, hemoptysis
General Treatment Approach: Directly Observed Therapy (DOTS) with multi-drug regimen (rifampicin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol) for 6-9 months, isolation initially
India-Specific Notes: High burden disease in India; free treatment under NTEP program; drug resistance concern

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe shortness of breath, rapid breathing, low oxygen levels, cyanosis
General Treatment Approach: Emergency oxygen therapy, mechanical ventilation if needed, treat underlying cause, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: Can be caused by multiple factors including pollution in urban India

Key Clinical Indicators: Sudden chest pain, shortness of breath, rapid heart rate, leg swelling
General Treatment Approach: Anticoagulation therapy (heparin/LMWH), thrombolytics if massive PE, oxygen therapy, ICU monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Risk increased with long travel, surgery, immobilization

Key Clinical Indicators: Blood glucose >250 mg/dL, confusion, abdominal pain, fruity breath, dehydration
General Treatment Approach: IV fluids, insulin therapy, electrolyte replacement, continuous monitoring, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: Common in Type 1 diabetes; can occur in Type 2 with stress/infection

Key Clinical Indicators: Very high glucose >600 mg/dL, severe dehydration, confusion, no ketones
General Treatment Approach: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, insulin therapy, electrolyte correction, ICU monitoring
India-Specific Notes: More common in elderly Type 2 diabetics; high mortality if untreated

Key Clinical Indicators: Glucose <50 mg/dL, confusion, sweating, loss of consciousness, seizures
General Treatment Approach: Immediate glucose administration (IV dextrose or glucagon injection), continuous monitoring, identify cause
India-Specific Notes: Common in insulin-treated diabetics; educate on recognition and prevention

Key Clinical Indicators: Very high temperature, rapid irregular heart rate, confusion, severe agitation
General Treatment Approach: Antithyroid medications, beta-blockers, corticosteroids, cooling measures, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: Rare but life-threatening; often triggered by infection or surgery

Key Clinical Indicators: Hemoglobin <7 g/dL, severe fatigue, shortness of breath, chest pain, pallor
General Treatment Approach: Blood transfusion, identify and treat underlying cause (iron, B12, folate deficiency or bleeding), hospitalization
India-Specific Notes: Very common in Indian women and children; nutritional deficiencies prevalent

Key Clinical Indicators: Platelet count <20000, bleeding gums, bruising, petechiae, internal bleeding risk
General Treatment Approach: Platelet transfusion, treat underlying cause, bleeding precautions, hospitalization
India-Specific Notes: Can be caused by dengue, medications, or autoimmune conditions

Key Clinical Indicators: Abnormal WBC count, fever, fatigue, easy bruising, bone pain, infections
General Treatment Approach: Comprehensive blood work, bone marrow biopsy, referral to oncology center, chemotherapy if confirmed
India-Specific Notes: Requires specialized cancer center; survival rates improving with modern therapy

Key Clinical Indicators: Fever, platelet count <50000, abdominal pain, vomiting, bleeding manifestations
General Treatment Approach: Hospitalization, IV fluid therapy, platelet monitoring, supportive care, no NSAIDs, strict monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Endemic in India during monsoon; NS1 antigen test for early diagnosis; no specific antiviral

Key Clinical Indicators: Fever/hypothermia, high WBC, confusion, rapid heart rate, low BP, organ dysfunction
General Treatment Approach: ICU admission, broad-spectrum IV antibiotics, IV fluids, vasopressor support, source control, organ support
India-Specific Notes: High mortality; early recognition and treatment crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: High fever with chills, confusion, anemia, jaundice, organ failure
General Treatment Approach: IV antimalarial therapy (artesunate), supportive care, ICU monitoring, blood transfusion if needed
India-Specific Notes: Endemic in many parts of India; rapid diagnostic tests available; P. falciparum most dangerous

Key Clinical Indicators: Prolonged high fever, abdominal pain, confusion, rose spots, relative bradycardia
General Treatment Approach: IV antibiotics (ceftriaxone/azithromycin), IV fluids, supportive care, hospitalization
India-Specific Notes: Common in India due to poor sanitation; blood culture for diagnosis; antibiotic resistance increasing

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe headache, high fever, neck stiffness, confusion, photophobia, rash
General Treatment Approach: Emergency IV antibiotics, steroids, lumbar puncture, ICU monitoring, isolation
India-Specific Notes: High mortality if delayed; viral vs bacterial differentiation crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: Fever, cough, severe shortness of breath, oxygen saturation <94%, chest pain
General Treatment Approach: Oxygen therapy, antivirals (remdesivir), steroids (dexamethasone), anticoagulation, respiratory support, ICU if needed
India-Specific Notes: Vaccination recommended; severity varies with variants; post-COVID complications common

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe upper abdominal pain radiating to back, nausea, vomiting, elevated amylase/lipase
General Treatment Approach: NPO (nothing by mouth), IV fluids, pain management, nutritional support, treat underlying cause, hospitalization
India-Specific Notes: Often alcohol or gallstone-related; can be severe in India due to delayed presentation

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe abdominal pain, rigid abdomen, fever, high WBC, signs of perforation
General Treatment Approach: Emergency surgery, IV antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, ICU care
India-Specific Notes: Surgical emergency; high mortality; often from appendicitis or peptic ulcer perforation

Key Clinical Indicators: Low hemoglobin, black/bloody stools, vomiting blood, low BP, rapid heart rate
General Treatment Approach: Emergency endoscopy, blood transfusion, IV proton pump inhibitors, hemostatic procedures, ICU monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Common causes: peptic ulcers, varices; H. pylori testing important

Key Clinical Indicators: Rising creatinine, decreased urine output, fluid overload, electrolyte imbalances
General Treatment Approach: IV fluids or fluid restriction, treat underlying cause, electrolyte management, possible dialysis
India-Specific Notes: Diabetes and dehydration common causes; early detection prevents chronic kidney disease

Key Clinical Indicators: High BP, anemia, electrolyte abnormalities, uremia symptoms, fluid retention
General Treatment Approach: Blood pressure control, anemia management, phosphate binders, dietary restrictions, dialysis preparation
India-Specific Notes: Growing epidemic in India; diabetes and hypertension main causes; dialysis/transplant needed

Key Clinical Indicators: Sudden severe headache, confusion, vomiting, high BP, neurological deficits
General Treatment Approach: Emergency CT scan, blood pressure control, neurosurgical evaluation, ICU monitoring, possible surgery
India-Specific Notes: Often related to uncontrolled hypertension; rapid intervention crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: Continuous seizures >5 minutes, loss of consciousness, convulsions
General Treatment Approach: Benzodiazepines, anticonvulsants, airway protection, IV access, ICU monitoring, identify trigger
India-Specific Notes: Requires immediate treatment; non-compliance with medications common cause

Key Clinical Indicators: Fever, confusion, seizures, severe headache, altered consciousness
General Treatment Approach: IV antivirals (acyclovir if HSV suspected), steroids, supportive care, ICU monitoring, lumbar puncture
India-Specific Notes: Japanese encephalitis common in India; vaccination available for high-risk areas

Key Clinical Indicators: Runny nose, sore throat, mild cough, low-grade fever, body aches
General Treatment Approach: Rest, hydration, symptomatic relief (decongestants, antihistamines), warm fluids, steam inhalation
India-Specific Notes: Very common; viral infection; antibiotics not needed; improves immunity

Key Clinical Indicators: High fever, cough, fatigue, muscle pain, headache
General Treatment Approach: Rest, hydration, antipyretics (paracetamol), antiviral medications if started early (oseltamivir), monitor for complications
India-Specific Notes: Annual vaccination recommended; can be severe in elderly/children

Key Clinical Indicators: Persistent cough, chest discomfort, mild fever, mucus production
General Treatment Approach: Rest, hydration, cough suppressants, bronchodilators if wheezing, usually viral (antibiotics only if bacterial)
India-Specific Notes: Common in winter and post-monsoon; pollution exacerbates symptoms in urban areas

Key Clinical Indicators: Sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes/nose, seasonal pattern
General Treatment Approach: Antihistamines, nasal corticosteroid sprays, allergen avoidance, saline nasal rinses
India-Specific Notes: Very common in India; dust mites, pollen triggers; air quality impacts severity

Key Clinical Indicators: Facial pain/pressure, headache, nasal congestion, thick nasal discharge
General Treatment Approach: Saline irrigation, decongestants, nasal corticosteroids, pain relievers, antibiotics if bacterial (10-14 days)
India-Specific Notes: Common post-cold complication; steam inhalation helps; chronic cases need further evaluation

Key Clinical Indicators: Upper abdominal pain, nausea, bloating, loss of appetite
General Treatment Approach: Proton pump inhibitors, H2 blockers, antacids, dietary modifications, avoid NSAIDs, H. pylori testing
India-Specific Notes: Very common in India; spicy food tolerance varies; H. pylori prevalence high

Key Clinical Indicators: Heartburn, chest discomfort, sore throat, regurgitation, worse when lying down
General Treatment Approach: Proton pump inhibitors, lifestyle modifications (weight loss, avoid triggers), elevate head while sleeping, dietary changes
India-Specific Notes: Chronic condition; obesity and diet-related; long-term management needed

Key Clinical Indicators: Abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea/constipation, symptom pattern, stress-related
General Treatment Approach: Dietary modifications (low FODMAP), fiber supplements, antispasmodics, probiotics, stress management, psychological support
India-Specific Notes: Diagnosis of exclusion; stress and diet major triggers; affects quality of life

Key Clinical Indicators: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, abdominal cramps, mild fever, dehydration risk
General Treatment Approach: Oral rehydration solutions (ORS), rest, gradual diet reintroduction, anti-emetics if severe, monitor for dehydration
India-Specific Notes: Common in monsoon season; contaminated water/food; hand hygiene crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: Discomfort in upper abdomen, nausea, bloating, fullness after eating
General Treatment Approach: Antacids, dietary modifications, smaller frequent meals, avoid trigger foods, over-the-counter medications
India-Specific Notes: Very common; lifestyle and dietary factors; stress-related

Key Clinical Indicators: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal cramps within hours of eating
General Treatment Approach: Hydration with ORS, rest, avoid solid food initially, bland diet, probiotics, medical attention if severe/persistent
India-Specific Notes: Common with street food; proper food handling and hygiene essential

Key Clinical Indicators: High fasting glucose (>126 mg/dL), family history, increased thirst/urination
General Treatment Approach: Lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise), oral hypoglycemics (metformin first-line), blood sugar monitoring, HbA1c tracking
India-Specific Notes: Epidemic in India; genetic predisposition high; early screening recommended age 30+

Key Clinical Indicators: Fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL, HbA1c 5.7-6.4%, lifestyle risk factors
General Treatment Approach: Intensive lifestyle modifications (weight loss 5-7%, exercise 150 min/week), dietary counseling, regular monitoring
India-Specific Notes: High prevalence in India; reversible with lifestyle changes; prevent progression to diabetes

Key Clinical Indicators: High cholesterol (>200 mg/dL), high LDL, low HDL, family history
General Treatment Approach: Statins, dietary modifications (low saturated fat), regular exercise, weight management, periodic lipid monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Common in urban India; diet transition to processed foods; cardiovascular risk factor

Key Clinical Indicators: High BP, high glucose, high cholesterol, abdominal obesity (waist >90cm men, >80cm women)
General Treatment Approach: Comprehensive lifestyle modification, medications for individual components, weight loss target, regular monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Growing problem in India; urbanization and lifestyle changes; increases CVD risk 2-3x

Key Clinical Indicators: Joint pain, stiffness worse with activity, age >45, joint swelling
General Treatment Approach: Pain relievers (paracetamol, NSAIDs), physiotherapy, weight management, joint protection strategies, topical analgesics
India-Specific Notes: Common in elderly; obesity increases risk; physiotherapy important for mobility

Key Clinical Indicators: Joint pain, morning stiffness >30 minutes, swelling, symmetrical pattern
General Treatment Approach: Disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs), NSAIDs, physiotherapy, regular monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Autoimmune condition; early treatment prevents joint damage; women more affected

Key Clinical Indicators: Localized muscle pain, tenderness, swelling, limited movement after exertion
General Treatment Approach: Rest, ice/heat therapy, pain relievers (paracetamol, NSAIDs), gentle stretching, gradual return to activity
India-Specific Notes: Common sports/overuse injury; proper warm-up prevents; physiotherapy for severe cases

Key Clinical Indicators: Lower back pain, muscle spasm, limited mobility, no neurological symptoms
General Treatment Approach: Pain relievers, muscle relaxants, physiotherapy, heat therapy, exercise, posture correction
India-Specific Notes: Very common; sedentary lifestyle and poor posture contribute; chronic cases need evaluation

Key Clinical Indicators: Widespread muscle pain, fatigue, poor sleep, tender points, chronic pattern
General Treatment Approach: Pain management (duloxetine, pregabalin), sleep improvement, exercise, cognitive behavioral therapy, stress management
India-Specific Notes: More common in women; diagnosis challenging; multidisciplinary approach needed

Key Clinical Indicators: Runny nose, sore throat, mild cough, low-grade fever, congestion
General Treatment Approach: Rest, hydration, symptomatic relief (decongestants, antihistamines), warm gargles, steam inhalation
India-Specific Notes: Very common; viral; antibiotics not indicated; hand hygiene prevents spread

Key Clinical Indicators: Sore throat, pain on swallowing, mild fever, red throat
General Treatment Approach: Pain relievers, warm salt water gargles, throat lozenges, hydration, antibiotics only if bacterial (strep throat)
India-Specific Notes: Common; mostly viral; strep throat needs antibiotics to prevent complications

Key Clinical Indicators: Sore throat, fever, swollen tonsils with white patches, difficulty swallowing
General Treatment Approach: Antibiotics if bacterial, pain relievers, antipyretics, rest, hydration, warm gargles
India-Specific Notes: Common in children; recurrent cases may need tonsillectomy

Key Clinical Indicators: Burning urination, frequent urination, lower abdominal discomfort, cloudy urine
General Treatment Approach: Antibiotics (nitrofurantoin, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole) 3-7 days, increased fluid intake, cranberry supplements
India-Specific Notes: Common in women; proper hygiene important; recurrent cases need evaluation

Key Clinical Indicators: Red eyes, discharge, itching, tearing, crusting of eyelids
General Treatment Approach: Antibiotic eye drops if bacterial, antihistamine drops if allergic, warm compresses, hygiene measures
India-Specific Notes: Highly contagious if viral/bacterial; avoid sharing towels; hand hygiene crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: BP 130-139/80-89 mmHg, usually asymptomatic, family history, lifestyle risk factors
General Treatment Approach: Lifestyle modifications initially (DASH diet, exercise, weight loss, salt restriction), single antihypertensive if needed, regular monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Silent killer; screening recommended age 30+; high prevalence in India; medication adherence crucial

Key Clinical Indicators: BP 120-129/<80 mmHg, no symptoms, risk factors present
General Treatment Approach: Intensive lifestyle modifications, weight loss, regular exercise, dietary changes, stress management, regular BP monitoring
India-Specific Notes: Preventive stage; lifestyle changes can reverse; regular monitoring prevents progression

Key Clinical Indicators: BP <90/60 mmHg, dizziness, fatigue, fainting episodes, usually constitutional
General Treatment Approach: Increase salt/fluid intake, compression stockings, small frequent meals, avoid sudden position changes, identify cause
India-Specific Notes: Common in young females; usually benign; rule out underlying causes

Key Clinical Indicators: Hemoglobin 10-12 g/dL, fatigue, pallor, weakness, dietary inadequacy
General Treatment Approach: Oral iron supplements (ferrous sulfate), vitamin C for absorption, dietary counseling (green leafy vegetables, jaggery), treat underlying cause
India-Specific Notes: Very common in Indian women and children; menstrual loss, poor diet; prevention through nutrition

Key Clinical Indicators: Low hemoglobin, fatigue, tingling in hands/feet, memory problems, dietary pattern (vegetarian)
General Treatment Approach: Vitamin B12 supplements (oral or injections), dietary modifications, fortified foods, treat underlying absorption issues
India-Specific Notes: Common in vegetarians; fortified foods important; neurological symptoms if prolonged

Key Clinical Indicators: Bilateral headache, tight band sensation, no nausea, stress-related, muscle tension
General Treatment Approach: Pain relievers (paracetamol, ibuprofen), stress management, adequate sleep, relaxation techniques, posture correction
India-Specific Notes: Most common headache; stress and screen time contribute; lifestyle modifications help

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe unilateral headache, nausea, photophobia, aura, family history
General Treatment Approach: Acute treatment (triptans, NSAIDs), preventive medications if frequent, trigger avoidance, lifestyle modifications
India-Specific Notes: Common; more in women; identify and avoid triggers; prophylactic treatment for frequent attacks

Key Clinical Indicators: Severe unilateral periorbital pain, tearing, nasal congestion, episodic pattern
General Treatment Approach: Acute: oxygen therapy, triptans; Preventive: verapamil, lithium; lifestyle modifications
India-Specific Notes: More common in men; smoking association; oxygen therapy very effective for acute attacks

Key Clinical Indicators: Spinning sensation with position changes, nausea, imbalance, normal neurological exam
General Treatment Approach: Vestibular exercises (Epley maneuver), anti-vertigo medications (betahistine), anti-emetics if needed
India-Specific Notes: Common; inner ear problem; exercises very effective; rule out central causes

Key Clinical Indicators: Itchy rash, redness, possible allergen exposure, no systemic symptoms
General Treatment Approach: Antihistamines, topical corticosteroids, allergen avoidance, cool compresses, moisturizers
India-Specific Notes: Common triggers: cosmetics, detergents, plants; identify and avoid allergens

Key Clinical Indicators: Itchy dry patches, redness, family history of allergies, chronic relapsing pattern
General Treatment Approach: Emollients, topical corticosteroids, antihistamines, avoid triggers, moisturize regularly
India-Specific Notes: Common in children; genetic component; environmental triggers; long-term management needed

Key Clinical Indicators: Red scaly patches, silvery scales, itching, nail changes, family history
General Treatment Approach: Topical treatments (corticosteroids, vitamin D analogs), phototherapy, systemic medications if severe, stress management
India-Specific Notes: Autoimmune condition; stress trigger; affects quality of life; newer biologics available

Key Clinical Indicators: Excessive worry, restlessness, palpitations, high stress levels, physical symptoms
General Treatment Approach: Cognitive behavioral therapy, relaxation techniques, SSRIs/SNRIs if moderate-severe, stress management, regular exercise
India-Specific Notes: Common; stigma in India; early intervention helps; combination therapy effective

Key Clinical Indicators: Persistent sadness, fatigue, loss of interest, sleep changes, poor concentration
General Treatment Approach: Psychotherapy (CBT), antidepressants (SSRIs) if moderate-severe, lifestyle modifications, social support
India-Specific Notes: Common but underdiagnosed in India; not just 'sadness'; treatment effective

Key Clinical Indicators: Multiple physical symptoms, high stress, anxiety, sleep disturbance, work pressure
General Treatment Approach: Stress management techniques, counseling, relaxation exercises, lifestyle modifications, possibly anxiolytics short-term
India-Specific Notes: Very common in urban India; work-life balance; meditation and yoga helpful

Key Clinical Indicators: Fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, constipation, elevated TSH, low T4
General Treatment Approach: Levothyroxine replacement, regular TSH monitoring, dose adjustments, lifelong therapy
India-Specific Notes: Common especially in women; iodine deficiency less common now; regular monitoring needed

Key Clinical Indicators: Weight loss, rapid heart rate, heat intolerance, tremor, anxiety, low TSH
General Treatment Approach: Antithyroid medications (methimazole), beta-blockers for symptoms, radioactive iodine or surgery for definitive treatment
India-Specific Notes: Less common than hypothyroidism; Graves' disease main cause; eye changes possible

Key Clinical Indicators: Fatigue, muscle pain, bone pain, frequent infections, low vitamin D levels
General Treatment Approach: Vitamin D supplementation (60,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks then maintenance), calcium, sun exposure
India-Specific Notes: Very common in India despite sunny climate; indoor lifestyle, clothing; affects bones and immunity

Key Clinical Indicators: BMI >30 kg/m², increased waist circumference, lifestyle factors, comorbidities
General Treatment Approach: Dietary modifications (calorie deficit), regular exercise (150+ min/week), behavioral therapy, medical management if needed
India-Specific Notes: Rising in urban India; increases risk of diabetes, hypertension, CVD; multidisciplinary approach

Key Clinical Indicators: Dry mouth, thirst, dark urine, dizziness, low BP if severe
General Treatment Approach: Oral rehydration (ORS, water, electrolyte drinks), rest, IV fluids if severe, treat underlying cause
India-Specific Notes: Common in summer; diarrhea/vomiting; prevention crucial; elderly at higher risk

Key Clinical Indicators: Poor sleep quality, fatigue, difficulty falling/staying asleep, lifestyle factors, snoring
General Treatment Approach: Sleep hygiene, cognitive behavioral therapy, treat underlying causes (OSA, anxiety), possibly short-term sleep aids
India-Specific Notes: Common with stress, shift work; screen time affects sleep; sleep apnea needs evaluation

Key Clinical Indicators: Persistent fatigue >6 months, post-exertional malaise, unrefreshing sleep, cognitive difficulties
General Treatment Approach: Graded exercise therapy, cognitive behavioral therapy, symptomatic management, treat comorbidities
India-Specific Notes: Diagnosis of exclusion; affects quality of life; multidisciplinary management

Key Clinical Indicators: High body temperature, profuse sweating, fatigue, headache, nausea, rapid pulse
General Treatment Approach: Move to cool place, hydration with ORS, cool compresses, rest, monitor for heat stroke
India-Specific Notes: Common in hot Indian summers; outdoor workers at risk; prevention with hydration

Key Clinical Indicators: Thirst, dry mouth, fatigue, dizziness, decreased urine output
General Treatment Approach: Increased fluid intake (water, ORS, coconut water), rest, avoid sun exposure
India-Specific Notes: Very common; summer heat, diarrhea; elderly and children vulnerable

Key Clinical Indicators: Various symptoms depending on deficiency, poor diet history, specific signs
General Treatment Approach: Targeted supplementation (iron, B12, vitamin D, etc.), dietary counseling, treat malabsorption if present
India-Specific Notes: Common in India; poor diet diversity; vegetarian diet risks; screening recommended
